summary: Contact Photo Update Bug Fix testing complete. All 10 backend tests passed (100%). The bug fix correctly implements: 1) PUT /contacts now processes base64 photos to generate thumbnails via _process_photo(), 2) Thumbnails are small (~3-5KB) for fast avatar loading, 3) Raw photo field is removed from contacts collection after processing, 4) High-res photos are stored in separate contact_photos collection, 5) EXIF auto-rotation is applied via ImageOps.exif_transpose(), 6) Full-res photos remain available via /photo/full endpoint for congrats cards.
critical_code_review_comments: EXIF auto-rotation correctly implemented at line 475: ImageOps.exif_transpose(img)
update_contact now properly processes photos at lines 219-236 using _process_photo()
Raw photo removed with update_dict.pop('photo', None) at line 226 to keep contacts lightweight
Frontend photo priority is correct at line 224: photo_thumbnail || photo_url || photo || null
High-res stored in contact_photos collection for congrats cards/emails via lines 228-233
seed_data_creation: Created temporary test contact during test_create_contact_then_update_photo (auto-cleaned up)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Photo bug fix verified in iteration 50. Key implementation: update_contact (PUT /contacts/{user_id}/{contact_id}) now calls _process_photo() when photo starts with 'data:' or is >1000 chars. _process_photo() applies ImageOps.exif_transpose() for EXIF rotation, generates 96x96 thumbnail (~3-5KB), and 1080px high-res. Thumbnail stored in photo_thumbnail field, high-res stored in contact_photos collection. Raw photo is popped from update_dict. /photo/full endpoint retrieves high-res for congrats cards.
